description: Code example imports from '@expo-ui/swift-ui' but frontmatter description and install instructions specify '@expo/ui/swift-ui'; these are different packages and the example import will fail at runtime.
suggested_fix: Change line 27 import to: import { Host, VStack, RNHostView } from "@expo/ui/swift-ui";

description: In-body reference links use './reference/' (singular) at lines 126, 127, 130, 133, 165 but the top-level References section at lines 18-22 uses './references/' (plural); the singular-form links point to a non-existent directory.
suggested_fix: Replace all './reference/' occurrences in the body text with './references/' to match the top-level References section.

pattern: unvalidated URL from argv passed to fetch
description: Script accepts any URL via process.argv[2] and makes an HTTP fetch request without validating the domain or scheme; an attacker supplying a crafted URL (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/) could trigger SSRF if the script is invoked outside the documented skill context.
suggested_fix: Add an allowlist of permitted URL prefixes (https://api.expo.dev/ and https://raw.githubusercontent.com/expo/) and throw before fetching if the URL does not match.

description: Dependency 'ajv' specified as '^8.17.1' allows automatic minor and patch upgrades; without a committed lockfile this could pull in a supply-chain-compromised version.
Dependency 'ajv-formats' specified as '^3.0.1' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.
Dependency 'js-yaml' specified as '^4.1.0' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI Jetpack Compose' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions (max 64 chars, lowercase, hyphens only).
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI SwiftUI' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions.

Commands reference '{baseDir}' (lines 19, 82, 84) with no explanation of whether Claude Code auto-substitutes this value or the agent must resolve it manually; a reader unfamiliar with the plugin system will not know how to run these commands.
suggested_fix: Add a note explaining that Claude Code substitutes {baseDir} with the skill directory path at load time, or replace with a relative path example.

description: Instruction 'its first child should almost always be a ScrollView' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always' without stating the exceptions; actionable guidance should be unconditional or enumerate the exceptions explicitly.
suggested_fix: State the rule unconditionally or list the specific exceptions: 'Use a ScrollView unless the route renders a FlatList or SectionList.'

description: Instruction 'it should almost always be the first component inside the route component' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always'.
suggested_fix: Replace with a conditional rule specifying when a ScrollView should not be first.

description: Instruction 'Whenever possible, include a <Link.Preview>' is vague — no criterion for when it is impossible or undesirable.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Include <Link.Preview> unless the destination route has no meaningful preview content.'

description: Instruction 'Use link previews frequently to enhance navigation' is vague — 'frequently' gives no actionable criterion.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Add Link.Preview to every Link that navigates to a detail screen.'

description: Rule 'Keep API routes focused — one responsibility per endpoint' — 'focused' is vague; the clarification 'one responsibility' partially rescues it but 'focused' adds no information.
suggested_fix: Remove 'focused'; keep only: 'One responsibility per endpoint.'

description: Rule 'Handle errors gracefully with try/catch' — 'gracefully' is a vague adverb with no actionable meaning in this context.
suggested_fix: Remove 'gracefully': 'Wrap handlers in try/catch; return structured error JSON with an appropriate HTTP status.'

description: Decision tree branch label 'Complex app -> React Query' uses 'complex' without defining the threshold; a developer cannot self-assess whether their app qualifies.
suggested_fix: Define the threshold, e.g.: 'Multiple data sources, background sync, or optimistic updates -> React Query'

description: CLAUDE.md Repository Structure section (lines 7-25) and install examples (lines 228-234) describe three separate top-level plugin directories (expo-app-design/, upgrading-expo/, expo-cicd-workflows/) that do not exist; all skills are consolidated under plugins/expo/ as a single plugin.
suggested_fix: Update Repository Structure and install examples to reflect the actual plugins/expo/ layout and the 'expo' plugin name.
